attribution.note: Curated 'further watching' playlist — links to third-party YouTube videos (not our content)

name: The Recursive Tarot — Further Watching
description: A companion PLAYLIST to history-of-tarot-sequence.json: external YouTube documentaries on the deep history of cards & games, tarot history, the Italian Renaissance, and the printing press / paper revolution. Grouped into four themed sections. Every video ID was verified embeddable via YouTube oEmbed on 2026-06-16; three candidates that disallowed embedding were dropped. Videos play full (start_sec:0, no end_sec) and advance when they end or when you press ⏭ — add an end_sec to crop any into a tight beat. Third-party videos remain the property of their channels.

items.name: Further Watching

metadata.card_subtitle: A companion playlist to The Recursive Tarot — real, verified documentaries on cards, tarot, the Renaissance & the printing press.

items.name: ① Cards & Games — the deep history
History of Playing Cards
Mamluk Cards → Bembo's Visconti Minor Arcana (influence)

sections.Our take: On-topic and accurate to this library: it traces the Mamluk pack (Topkapı) into Bonifacio Bembo's Visconti-Sforza minor arcana — both decks are here (`mamluk-deck`, `visconti-sforza-tarot`).
sections.Where we agree: The four suits — coins, cups, swords, sticks — are the Islamic **Mamluk** inheritance Europe copied; the **Visconti-Sforza** (c. 1451, Milan) is the oldest near-complete tarot.
sections.A caveat: The bright deck shown is a **modern reconstruction / repaint**, not the faded originals — and in the surviving Topkapı pack the **9 and 10 of swords are reconstructed replacement cards**, so their depiction is interpretive, not artifact.

items.name: ② Tarot History — from game to occult
The Ultimate Tarot History — Part I: From Triumphs to the Tarot

sections.Our take: A lively, well-made overview — and it carries our central thesis: tarot began as a Renaissance **game**, not divination, with the trumps drawn from Petrarch's *Trionfi* and the triumphal processions; the occult reading came centuries later.
sections.Where we agree: Game first, divination added later (18th-c. France) · the trumps descend from triumphal / Petrarchan processions · the name comes from the **game** (*tarocchi* / *taroco*), not from Egypt or Atlantis · the Visconti court and the Cary-Yale (1441) are pivotal.
sections.Where we differ: The video calls the **'Mantegna Tarocchi' the 'missing link'** to tarot. We hold the opposite, carefully: the Mantegna engravings are **not a tarot and not a card game** — a didactic 'Ladder of Being'; the name is a later misattribution and the tarot-lineage claim is **contested, not settled**. We also treat the lost **Marziano da Tortona** deck (c. 1425) and several of the dates as debated reconstructions.

items.name: The Ultimate Tarot History — Part II: From Games to the Occult & Beyond
A History of the Tarot
Michael Dummett on the Tarot
Tarot History: Theories of Origin
The Hidden Secrets of the Tarot of Marseille (ARTE)

sections.Our take: A beautiful, scholarly-feeling film — but it slides from solid history into one **contested hypothesis** and stages it like a thriller reveal. Watch for the art; read the central claim sceptically.
sections.Where we agree: The sober parts (from card-historian Thierry Depaulis) are right: tarot is **Italian, 15th-century**, and **'Tarot de Marseille' is a late naming convention** from the city that printed the pattern. The trumps do swim in Renaissance triumph / allegory / Neoplatonic imagery. Game first, not an occult manual.
sections.Where we differ: Its core claim — that **Marsilio Ficino designed the trumps as a hidden Platonic teaching** (Chariot = Plato's chariot of the soul, Devil = the Cave + Hell, World = the Orphic soul) — is **Christophe Poncet's interpretation, not scholarly consensus**. The film proves visual *resemblance*, not authorship. **'Marseille = Marsile [Ficin]' is a pun, not an etymology.** And the standard Marseille pattern crystallized in **17th–18th-century Provence**, not 1480s Florence — centuries and regions away from Ficino. The Esztergom 'Botticelli' Temperance is itself disputed.
sections.The deeper point: This documentary is itself a spectacular act of **meaning *added* to the cards** — a learned descendant of Court de Gébelin's 'it was always secretly esoteric.' Held as **interpretation, not origin-history**, it's a treasure — and exactly the meaning-making recursive.eco studies.
